contentBlocks.original:
अनन्तसंसार समुद्रतार नौकायिताभ्यां गुरुभक्तिदाभ्याम् ।
वैराग्यसाम्राज्यदपूजनाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantasaṃsāra samudratāra naukāyitābhyāṃ gurubhaktidābhyām ।
vairāgyasāmrājyadapūjanābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which is a boat, which helps me,cross the endless ocean of life,
Which endows me, with the sense of devotion to my Guru,
And by worship of which, I attain the dominion of renunciation.
contentBlocks.meanings: अनन्तसंसार (anantasaṃsāra) – endless cycle of life (ananta:endless | saṃsāra:cycle of life); समुद्रतार (samudratāra) – ocean-crossing (samudra:ocean | tāra:crossing); नौकायिताभ्यां (naukāyitābhyām) – like a boat (naukā:boat | yitābhyām:like); गुरुभक्तिदाभ्याम् (gurubhaktidābhyām) – endowing devotion to Guru (guru:Guru | bhakti:devotion | dābhyām:endowing); वैराग्यसाम्राज्यदपूजनाभ्यां (vairāgyasāmrājyadapūjanābhyām) – worship for renunciation dominion (vairāgya:renunciation | sāmrājya:dominion | da:for | pūjanābhyām:worship); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukābhyām:to the sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
कवित्ववाराशिनिशाकराभ्यां दौर्भाग्यदावां बुदमालिकाभ्याम् ।
दूरिकृतानम्र विपत्ततिभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kavitvavārāśiniśākarābhyāṃ daurbhāgyadāvāṃ budamālikābhyām ।
dūrikṛtānamra vipattatibhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which is the ocean of knowledge, resembling the full moon,
Which is the water, which puts out the fire of misfortunes,
And which removes distresses of those who prostrate before it.
contentBlocks.meanings: कवित्ववाराशिनिशाकराभ्यां (kavitvavārāśiniśākarābhyām) – to the moon of the ocean of poetry (kavitva:poetry | vārāśi:ocean | niśākara:moon | abhyām:to the two); दौर्भाग्यदावां (daurbhāgyadāvām) – forest fire of misfortune (daurbhāgya:misfortune | dāva:forest fire); बुदमालिकाभ्याम् (budamālikābhyām) – to the garland of wisdom (buda:wisdom | mālikā:garland | abhyām:to the two); दूरिकृतानम्र (dūrikṛtānamra) – removing the bowed (dūrikṛta:removing | anamra:the bowed); विपत्ततिभ्यां (vipattatibhyām) – to the series of calamities (vipatti:calamity | tati:series | abhyām:to the two); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of the revered Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukā:sandals | abhyām:to the two);

contentBlocks.original:
नता ययोः श्रीपतितां समीयुः कदाचिदप्याशु दरिद्रवर्याः ।
मूकाश्र्च वाचस्पतितां हि ताभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
natā yayoḥ śrīpatitāṃ samīyuḥ kadācidapyāśu daridravaryāḥ ।
mūkāśrca vācaspatitāṃ hi tābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which make those who prostrate before it,
Possessors of great wealth, even if they are very poor,
And which makes even dumb people in to great orators.
contentBlocks.meanings: नता (natā) – bowed; ययोः (yayoḥ) – of which; श्रीपतिताम् (śrīpatitām) – wealthy state (śrī:wealth | patitām:fallen into); समीयुः (samīyuḥ) – attained; कदाचित् (kadācid) – sometimes; अपि (api) – even; आशु (āśu) – quickly; दरिद्रवर्याः (daridravaryāḥ) – great poor ones (daridra:poor | varyāḥ:great ones); मूकाः (mūkāḥ) – dumb; च (ca) – and; वाचस्पतिताम् (vācaspatitām) – eloquence (vācas:speech | patitām:fallen into); हि (hi) – indeed; ताभ्यां (tābhyām) – to those; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukābhyām:to the sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
नालीकनीकाश पदाहृताभ्यां नानाविमोहादि निवारिकाभ्याम् ।
नमज्जनाभीष्टततिप्रदाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nālīkanīkāśa padāhṛtābhyāṃ nānāvimohādi nivārikābhyām ।
namajjanābhīṣṭatatipradābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which attracts us, to lotus like feet of our Guru,
Which cures us, of the unwanted desires,
And which helps fulfill the desires of those who salute.
contentBlocks.meanings: नालीकनीकाश (nālīkanīkāśa) – lotus-like (nālīka:lotus | nīkāśa:like); पदाहृताभ्यां (padāhṛtābhyām) – attracted to the feet (pada:feet | āhṛta:attracted); नानाविमोहादि (nānāvimohādi) – various delusions etc. (nānā:various | vimohādi:delusions etc.); निवारिकाभ्याम् (nivārikābhyām) – removing; नमज्जनाभीष्टततिप्रदाभ्यां (namajjanābhīṣṭatatipradābhyām) – fulfilling devotees' desires (namajjana:devotees | abhīṣṭa:desires | tati:multitude | pradā:fulfilling); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukā:sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
नृपालि मौलिव्रजरत्नकान्ति सरिद्विराजत् झषकन्यकाभ्याम् ।
नृपत्वदाभ्यां नतलोकपङ्कते: नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nṛpāli maulivrajaratnakānti saridvirājat jhaṣakanyakābhyām ।
nṛpatvadābhyāṃ natalokapaṅkate: namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which shine like gems on the crown of a king,
Which shine like a maid in the crocodile infested stream,
And which make the devotees attain the status of a king.
contentBlocks.meanings: नृपालि (nṛpāli) – king's; मौलिव्रजरत्नकान्ति (maulivrajaratnakānti) – shine like crown jewels (mauli:crown | vraja:group | ratna:jewel | kānti:shine); सरिद्विराजत् (saridvirājat) – shine in the river (sarit:river | virājat:shine); झषकन्यकाभ्याम् (jhaṣakanyakābhyām) – like a maid in stream (jhaṣa:crocodile | kanyaka:maid | abhyām:by two); नृपत्वदाभ्यां (nṛpatvadābhyāṃ) – attain the status of king (nṛpatva:status of king | dābhyām:by two); नतलोकपङ्कते: (natalokapaṅkateḥ) – to the devotees (nata:bowed | loka:people | paṅkateḥ:to the group); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of Guru (śrī:revered | guru:teacher | pādukābhyām:to the sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
पापान्धकारार्क परम्पराभ्यां तापत्रयाहीन्द्र खगेश्र्वराभ्याम् ।
जाड्याब्धि संशोषण वाडवाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpāndhakārārka paramparābhyāṃ tāpatrayāhīndra khageśrvarābhyām ।
jāḍyābdhi saṃśoṣaṇa vāḍavābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which is like a series of Suns, driving away the dark sins,
Which is like the king of eagles, driving away the cobra of miseries,
And which is like a terrific fire drying away the ocean of ignorance.
contentBlocks.meanings: पापान्धकारार्क (pāpāndhakārārka) – sun of dark sins (pāpa:sins | andhakāra:darkness | arka:sun); परम्पराभ्यां (paramparābhyām) – series of; तापत्रयाहीन्द्र (tāpatrayāhīndra) – king of eagles (tāpatraya:threefold miseries | ahī:snake | indra:king); खगेश्र्वराभ्याम् (khageśvarābhyām) – lord of birds (khaga:bird | īśvara:lord); जाड्याब्धि (jāḍyābdhi) – ocean of ignorance (jāḍya:ignorance | abdhi:ocean); संशोषण (saṃśoṣaṇa) – drying; वाडवाभ्यां (vāḍavābhyām) – terrific fire (vāḍava:fire); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of Guru (śrī:revered | guru:teacher | pādukā:sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
शमादिषट्क प्रदवैभवाभ्यां समाधिदान व्रतदीक्षिताभ्याम् ।
रमाधवान्ध्रिस्थिरभक्तिदाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śamādiṣaṭka pradavaibhavābhyāṃ samādhidāna vratadīkṣitābhyām ।
ramādhavāndhristhirabhaktidābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru,
Which endows us with the glorious six qualities like sham,
Which gives the students the ability to go in to eternal trance,
And which helps to get perennial devotion to the feet of Vishnu.
contentBlocks.meanings: शमादिषट्क (śamādiṣaṭka) – six qualities like sham (śama:sham | ādi:beginning with | ṣaṭka:group of six); प्रदवैभवाभ्यां (pradavaibhavābhyām) – endows with glorious qualities (prada:endowing | vaibhava:glory); समाधिदान (samādhidāna) – gives eternal trance (samādhi:eternal trance | dāna:giving); व्रतदीक्षिताभ्याम् (vratadīkṣitābhyām) – initiated in vows (vrata:vow | dīkṣita:initiated); रमाधवान्ध्रिस्थिरभक्तिदाभ्यां (ramādhavāndhristhirabhaktidābhyām) – gives devotion to Vishnu's feet (ramādhava:Vishnu | andhri:feet | sthira:steady | bhakti:devotion | dā:giving); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of my Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukā:sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
स्वार्चापराणाम् अखिलेष्टदाभ्यां स्वाहासहायाक्षधुरन्धराभ्याम् ।
स्वान्ताच्छभावप्रदपूजनाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svārcāparāṇām akhileṣṭadābhyāṃ svāhāsahāyākṣadhurandharābhyām ।
svāntācchabhāvapradapūjanābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru
Which bestows all desires of the serving disciples,
Who are ever involved in carrying the burden of service
And which helps the aspirants to the state of realization.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्वार्चापराणाम् (svārcāparāṇām) – of those devoted to worship (svā:own | arcā:worship | parāṇām:of those devoted); अखिलेष्टदाभ्यां (akhileṣṭadābhyām) – granting all desires (akhila:all | iṣṭa:desires | dābhyām:granting); स्वाहासहायाक्षधुरन्धराभ्याम् (svāhāsahāyākṣadhurandharābhyām) – supporting the burden of service (svāhā:offering | sahāya:help | akṣa:axis | dhurandhara:bearing the burden); स्वान्ताच्छभावप्रदपूजनाभ्यां (svāntācchabhāvapradapūjanābhyām) – bestowing pure nature worship (svānta:inner self | accha:pure | bhāva:nature | prada:bestowing | pūjanābhyām:worship); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of the Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukābhyām:to the sandals);

contentBlocks.original:
कामादिसर्प व्रजगारुडाभ्यां विवेकवैराग्य निधिप्रदाभ्याम् ।
बोधप्रदाभ्यां दृतमोक्षदाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmādisarpa vrajagāruḍābhyāṃ vivekavairāgya nidhipradābhyām ।
bodhapradābhyāṃ dṛtamokṣadābhyāṃ namo namaḥ śrīgurupādukābhyām ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the sandals of my Guru
Which is the Garuda, which drives away the serpent of passion,
Which provides one, with the treasure of wisdom and renunciation,
Which blesses one, with enlightened knowledge,
And blesses the aspirant with speedy salvation.
contentBlocks.meanings: कामादिसर्प (kāmādisarpa) – serpent of passion etc. (kāma:passion | ādi:etc. | sarpa:serpent); व्रजगारुडाभ्यां (vrajagāruḍābhyām) – Garuda driving away (vraja:driving away | garuḍa:Garuda | ābhyām:by the two); विवेकवैराग्य (vivekavairāgya) – wisdom and renunciation (viveka:wisdom | vairāgya:renunciation); निधिप्रदाभ्याम् (nidhipradābhyām) – providing treasure (nidhi:treasure | pradā:providing | ābhyām:by the two); बोधप्रदाभ्यां (bodhapradābhyām) – giving enlightenment (bodha:enlightenment | pradā:giving | ābhyām:by the two); दृतमोक्षदाभ्यां (dṛtamokṣadābhyām) – granting speedy salvation (dṛta:speedy | mokṣa:salvation | dā:granting | ābhyām:by the two); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम् (śrīgurupādukābhyām) – to the sandals of Guru (śrī:revered | guru:Guru | pādukā:sandals | ābhyām:to the two);
